# Markets Committee
*Markets Committee · November 4-6, 2025*

## Key Takeaways

- **CAR-SA vote delayed** to December after stakeholder pushback on the ORTP floor methodology.
- DRR aggregation rules advance with **unanimous** support; FERC filing expected Q1 2026.
- Tariff cleanup items 8-11 passed on consent with no discussion.

## Executive Summary

**Key Developments**

The committee spent most of day one on capacity auction reform. ISO-NE signalled a Q4 filing target despite the delayed vote.

- NEPOOL counsel flagged a compliance question on Order 2023 timelines.
- The IMM presented winter energy market metrics showing muted scarcity pricing.

## Agenda Item Summaries

## 3 — Capacity Auction Reforms – Seasonal/Accreditation

### 3.a — ORTP Floor Proposal

ISO-NE presented the revised Offer Review Trigger Price floor. Stakeholders questioned the treatment of **hybrid resources** under the new methodology.

#### Study assumptions

The study holds tie benefits constant at 2024 values.

**Data: ORTP floor by technology**
| Technology | Floor ($/kW-mo) |
| --- | --- |
| Solar PV | 0.00 |
| Battery (4h) | 2.41 |

**Next Steps:**
- Revised proposal returns in December
- Written comments due November 21

### 3.b — Accreditation Transition Schedule

> [!Watch] The transition schedule interacts with the FCA 20 qualification deadline.

The proposed two-auction transition drew support from generators and opposition from consumer advocates.

## 7 — Demand Response Aggregation

The DRR aggregation package passed unanimously and moves to the Participants Committee.

**Next Steps:** File with FERC in Q1 2026; implementation guide to follow.
